speaker:
TYRIAN
               
              SAILOR
text: Where is Lord Helicanus? He can resolve you.

speaker:
TYRIAN
               
              SAILOR
text: O, here he is.—

speaker:
TYRIAN
               
              SAILOR
text: Sir, there is a barge put off from Mytilene,

speaker:
TYRIAN
               
              SAILOR
text: And in it is Lysimachus, the Governor,

speaker:
TYRIAN
               
              SAILOR
text: Who craves to come aboard. What is your will?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: That he have his.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Call up some gentlemen.

speaker:
TYRIAN
               
              SAILOR
text: Ho, gentlemen, my lord calls.

text: Doth your Lordship call?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Gentlemen,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: There is some of worth would come aboard.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: I pray, greet him fairly.

SAILOR

text: Sir,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Hail, reverend sir. The gods preserve you.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: And you, to outlive the age I am,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: And die as I would do.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: You wish me well.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Being on shore, honoring of Neptune’s triumphs,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Seeing this goodly vessel ride before us,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: I made to it to know of whence you are.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: First, what is your place?

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: I am the governor of this place you lie before.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Sir,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Our vessel is of Tyre, in it the King,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: A man who for this three months hath not spoken

speaker: HELICANUS
text: To anyone, nor taken sustenance

speaker: HELICANUS
text: But to prorogue his grief.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Upon what ground is his distemperature?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: ’Twould be too tedious to repeat,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: But the main grief springs from the loss

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Of a belovèd daughter and a wife.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: May we not see him?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: You may,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: But bootless is your sight. He will not speak

speaker: HELICANUS
text: To any.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Yet let me obtain my wish.

speaker: HELICANUS
text:
Behold him.
                 
                Pericles
                 
                is
                 
                revealed

               This was a goodly person,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Till the disaster that one mortal night

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Drove him to this.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Sir king, all hail! The gods preserve you. Hail,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Royal sir!

speaker: HELICANUS
text: It is in vain; he will not speak to you.

speaker: LORD
text: Sir, we have a maid in Mytilene,

speaker: LORD
text: I durst wager would win some words of him.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: ’Tis well bethought.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: She, questionless, with her sweet harmony

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: And other chosen attractions, would allure

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: And make a batt’ry through his defended ports,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Which now are midway stopped.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: She is all happy as the fairest of all,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: And, with her fellow maid, is now upon

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: The leafy shelter that abuts against

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: The island’s side.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Sure, all effectless; yet nothing we’ll omit

speaker: HELICANUS
text: That bears recovery’s name.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: But since your kindness

speaker: HELICANUS
text: We have stretched thus far, let us beseech you

speaker: HELICANUS
text: That for our gold we may provision have,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Wherein we are not destitute for want,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: But weary for the staleness.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: O, sir, a courtesy

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Which, if we should deny, the most just God

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: For every graft would send a caterpillar,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: And so inflict our province. Yet once more

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Let me entreat to know at large the cause

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Of your king’s sorrow.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Sit, sir, I will recount it to you. But see,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: I am prevented.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: O, here’s the lady that I sent for.—

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Welcome, fair one.—Is ’t not a goodly presence?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: She’s a gallant lady.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: She’s such a one that, were I well assured

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Came of a gentle kind and noble stock,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: I’d wish no better choice, and think me rarely wed.—

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Fair one, all goodness that consists in beauty:

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Expect even here, where is a kingly patient,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: If that thy prosperous and artificial feat

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Can draw him but to answer thee in aught,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Thy sacred physic shall receive such pay

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: As thy desires can wish.

speaker: MARINA
text: Sir, I will use

speaker: MARINA
text: My utmost skill in his recovery, provided

speaker: MARINA
text: That none but I and my companion maid

speaker: MARINA
text: Be suffered to come near him.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Come, let us

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Leave her, and the gods make her prosperous.

speaker: MARINA
text: sings

text: Marked he your music?

speaker: MARINA
text: No, nor looked on us.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: See, she will speak to him.

speaker: MARINA
text: Hail, sir! My lord, lend ear.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Hum, ha!

speaker: MARINA
text: I am a maid, my lord,

speaker: MARINA
text: That ne’er before invited eyes, but have

speaker: MARINA
text: Been gazed on like a comet. She speaks,

speaker: MARINA
text: My lord, that may be hath endured a grief

speaker: MARINA
text: Might equal yours, if both were justly weighed.

speaker: MARINA
text: Though wayward Fortune did malign my state,

speaker: MARINA
text: My derivation was from ancestors

speaker: MARINA
text: Who stood equivalent with mighty kings.

speaker: MARINA
text: But time hath rooted out my parentage,

speaker: MARINA
text: And to the world and awkward casualties

text:
Bound me in servitude.

               I will desist,

speaker: MARINA
text: But there is something glows upon my cheek,

speaker: MARINA
text: And whispers in mine ear “Go not till he speak.”

speaker: PERICLES
text: My fortunes—parentage—good parentage,

speaker: PERICLES
text: To equal mine! Was it not thus? What say you?

speaker: MARINA
text: I said, my lord, if you did know my parentage,

speaker: MARINA
text: You would not do me violence.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I do think so.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Pray you turn your eyes upon me.

speaker: PERICLES
text: You’re like something that—What countrywoman?

speaker: PERICLES
text: Here of these shores?

speaker: MARINA
text: No, nor of any shores.

speaker: MARINA
text: Yet I was mortally brought forth, and am

speaker: MARINA
text: No other than I appear.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I am great with woe, and shall deliver weeping.

speaker: PERICLES
text: My dearest wife was like this maid, and such

speaker: PERICLES
text: A one my daughter might have been: my queen’s

speaker: PERICLES
text: Square brows, her stature to an inch;

speaker: PERICLES
text: As wandlike straight, as silver-voiced; her eyes

speaker: PERICLES
text: As jewel-like, and cased as richly; in pace

speaker: PERICLES
text: Another Juno; who starves the ears she feeds

speaker: PERICLES
text: And makes them hungry the more she gives them speech.—

speaker: PERICLES
text: Where do you live?

speaker: MARINA
text: Where I am but a stranger.

speaker: MARINA
text: From the deck you may discern the place.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Where were you bred? And how achieved you these

speaker: PERICLES
text: Endowments which you make more rich to owe?

speaker: MARINA
text: If I should tell my history, it would seem

speaker: MARINA
text: Like lies disdained in the reporting.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Prithee, speak.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Falseness cannot come from thee, for thou lookest

speaker: PERICLES
text: Modest as Justice, and thou seemest a palace

speaker: PERICLES
text: For the crownèd Truth to dwell in. I will believe thee

speaker: PERICLES
text: And make my senses credit thy relation

speaker: PERICLES
text: To points that seem impossible, for thou lookest

speaker: PERICLES
text: Like one I loved indeed. What were thy friends?

speaker: PERICLES
text: Didst thou not say, when I did push thee back—

speaker: PERICLES
text: Which was when I perceived thee—that thou cam’st

speaker: PERICLES
text: From good descending?

speaker: MARINA
text: So indeed I did.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Report thy parentage. I think thou said’st

text: Thou hadst been tossed from wrong to injury,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And that thou thought’st thy griefs might equal mine,

speaker: PERICLES
text: If both were opened.

text: Some such thing I said,

speaker: MARINA
text: And said no more but what my thoughts

speaker: MARINA
text: Did warrant me was likely.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Tell thy story.

speaker: PERICLES
text: If thine considered prove the thousand part

speaker: PERICLES
text: Of my endurance, thou art a man, and I

speaker: PERICLES
text: Have suffered like a girl. Yet thou dost look

speaker: PERICLES
text: Like Patience gazing on kings’ graves and smiling

speaker: PERICLES
text: Extremity out of act. What were thy friends?

speaker: PERICLES
text: How lost thou them? Thy name, my most kind virgin,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Recount, I do beseech thee. Come, sit by me.

speaker: MARINA
text: My name is Marina.

speaker: PERICLES
text: O, I am mocked,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And thou by some incensèd god sent hither

speaker: PERICLES
text: To make the world to laugh at me!

speaker: MARINA
text: Patience, good sir,

speaker: MARINA
text: Or here I’ll cease.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Nay, I’ll be patient.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thou little know’st how thou dost startle me

speaker: PERICLES
text: To call thyself Marina.

speaker: MARINA
text: The name

speaker: MARINA
text: Was given me by one that had some power—

speaker: MARINA
text: My father, and a king.

text: How, a king’s daughter?

speaker: PERICLES
text: And called Marina?

speaker: MARINA
text: You said you would believe me.

speaker: MARINA
text: But not to be a troubler of your peace,

speaker: MARINA
text: I will end here.

text: But are you flesh and blood?

speaker: PERICLES

speaker: PERICLES
text: Motion? Well, speak on. Where were you born?

speaker: PERICLES
text: And wherefore called Marina?

speaker: MARINA
text: Called Marina

speaker: MARINA
text: For I was born at sea.

speaker: PERICLES
text: At sea? What mother?

speaker: MARINA
text: My mother was the daughter of a king,

speaker: MARINA
text: Who died the minute I was born,

speaker: MARINA
text: As my good nurse Lychorida hath oft

speaker: MARINA
text: Delivered weeping.

text: O, stop there a little!

Aside

speaker: PERICLES
text: Did mock sad fools withal. This cannot be

speaker: PERICLES
text: My daughter, buried.—Well, where were you bred?

text: I’ll hear you more, to the bottom of your story,

text: You scorn. Believe me, ’twere best I did give o’er.

text: I will believe you by the syllable

text: Of what you shall deliver. Yet give me leave:

speaker: PERICLES
text: How came you in these parts? Where were you bred?

speaker: MARINA
text: The King my father did in Tarsus leave me,

speaker: MARINA
text: Till cruel Cleon with his wicked wife

speaker: MARINA
text: Did seek to murder me; and having wooed a villain

text: To attempt it, who, having drawn to do ’t,

speaker: MARINA
text: A crew of pirates came and rescued me,

speaker: MARINA
text: Brought me to Mytilene—But, good sir,

speaker: MARINA
text: Whither will you have me? Why do you weep?

speaker: MARINA
text: It may be you think me an impostor.

speaker: MARINA
text: No, good faith.

speaker: MARINA
text: I am the daughter to King Pericles,

speaker: MARINA
text: If good King Pericles be.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Ho, Helicanus!

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Calls my lord?

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thou art a grave and noble counselor,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Most wise in general. Tell me, if thou canst,

speaker: PERICLES
text: What this maid is, or what is like to be,

speaker: PERICLES
text: That thus hath made me weep.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: I know not;

speaker: HELICANUS
text: But here’s the regent, sir, of Mytilene

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Speaks nobly of her.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: She never would tell

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Her parentage. Being demanded that,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: She would sit still and weep.

speaker: PERICLES
text: O, Helicanus! Strike me, honored sir.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Give me a gash, put me to present pain,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Lest this great sea of joys rushing upon me

speaker: PERICLES
text: O’erbear the shores of my mortality

speaker: PERICLES
text: And drown me with their sweetness.—O, come hither,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thou that beget’st him that did thee beget,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thou that wast born at sea, buried at Tarsus,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And found at sea again!—O, Helicanus,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Down on thy knees! Thank the holy gods as loud

speaker: PERICLES
text: As thunder threatens us. This is Marina.—

text: What was thy mother’s name? Tell me but that,

speaker: PERICLES
text: For truth can never be confirmed enough,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Though doubts did ever sleep.

speaker: MARINA
text: First, sir, I pray, what is your title?

text: I am Pericles of Tyre. But tell me now

text: My drowned queen’s name, as in the rest you said

text: Thou hast been godlike perfect, the heir of kingdoms,

text: And another life to Pericles thy father.

text: Is it no more to be your daughter than

text: To say my mother’s name was Thaisa?

text: Thaisa was my mother, who did end

text: The minute I began.

text: Now, blessing on thee! Rise. Thou ’rt my child.—

text: She is not dead at Tarsus, as she should

text: Have been, by savage Cleon. She shall tell thee all,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Who, hearing of your melancholy state,

text: Did come to see you.

text: I embrace you.—

text: Give me my robes.—I am wild in my beholding.

speaker: PERICLES
text: O heavens bless my girl! But hark, what music?

speaker: PERICLES
text: Tell Helicanus, my Marina, tell him o’er

speaker: PERICLES
text: Point by point, for yet he seems to doubt,

speaker: PERICLES
text: How sure you are my daughter.—But what music?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: My lord, I hear none.

speaker: PERICLES
text: None?

speaker: PERICLES
text: The music of the spheres!—List, my Marina.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: It is not good to cross him. Give him way.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Rarest sounds! Do you not hear?

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Music, my lord? I hear—

speaker: PERICLES
text: Most heavenly music.

speaker: PERICLES
text: It nips me unto list’ning, and thick slumber

speaker: PERICLES
text: Hangs upon mine eyes. Let me rest.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: A pillow for his head. So, leave him all.

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Well, my companion friends, if this but answer

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: To my just belief, I’ll well remember you.

speaker: DIANA
text: My temple stands in Ephesus. Hie thee thither

speaker: DIANA
text: And do upon mine altar sacrifice.

speaker: DIANA
text: There, when my maiden priests are met together,

speaker: DIANA
text: Before the people all,

speaker: DIANA
text: Reveal how thou at sea didst lose thy wife.

speaker: DIANA
text: To mourn thy crosses, with thy daughter’s, call,

speaker: DIANA
text: And give them repetition to the life.

speaker: DIANA
text: Or perform my bidding, or thou livest in woe;

speaker: DIANA
text: Do ’t, and happy, by my silver bow.

speaker: DIANA
text: Awake, and tell thy dream.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Celestial Dian,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Goddess argentine, I will obey thee.—

text: Helicanus!

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Sir.

text: The inhospitable Cleon, but I am

text: As our intents will need?

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: Sir,

speaker: LYSIMACHUS
text: With all my heart. And when you come ashore,

text: I have another suit.
